commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=34f30cad978170b181c50b46ee6bb9fdf0124c49)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Gemini web search `freshness` shortcuts from hard `timeRangeFilter` values to prompt-level recency hints, updates cache keying, docs, and tests, and folds two lint-only fixes in agent/Codex files.

PR surface: Source -1, Tests +42, Docs +2. Total +43 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction is attached to this PR. Source inspection confirms current main and v2026.6.9 send shortcut freshness as a hard `timeRangeFilter`, but the exact reported same-day endpoint rejection is only narrated in the PR body.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is maintainer judgment and contributor proof for changing documented Gemini freshness semantics, not a safe mechanical repair task.

target.title: Fix Gemini web search freshness handling
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Gemini-backed `web_search` could fail when callers passed relative `freshness` values like `day`. OpenClaw mapped those shortcuts to Gemini `google_search.timeRangeFilter`, but the Gemini endpoint rejects some relative hard ranges, including the same-day shape seen in production, before any grounded result is returned.

The same CI run also exposed two current-main lint/type blockers outside the Gemini files:

- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-result-context-guard.ts` imported `CONTEXT_LIMIT_TRUNCATION_NOTICE` without using it.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` passed an async helper directly to a void callback slot.

## Why This Change Was Made

This keeps explicit `date_after` / `date_before` behavior as the hard Gemini Google Search grounding time range path, while changing relative `freshness` shortcuts to a query-level recency instruction. That preserves the user's intent to prioritize recent sources without sending an invalid hard filter to Gemini.

The cache key now includes the freshness shortcut even though the hard filter is omitted, so cached responses for the same query remain separated by recency intent.

The lint/type blockers are kept as narrow mechanical fixes in the same PR so the CI gate can evaluate this branch without a second companion PR.

## User Impact

Users can keep calling Gemini `web_search` with `freshness: "day"`, `week`, `month`, or `year` without the request being rejected by Gemini's time range validation. When a hard grounding range is required, users can still use `date_after` and `date_before`.

No runtime behavior change is intended from the lint/type cleanup.

## Evidence

Validated on top of `origin/main@4e2f0157c7`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/google/web-search-provider.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-result-context-guard.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/google/web-search-provider.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/google/src/gemini-web-search-provider.ts extensions/google/src/gemini-web-search-provider.runtime.ts extensions/google/web-search-provider.test.ts docs/tools/gemini-search.md docs/tools/web.md extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-result-context-guard.ts`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/tools/gemini-search.md docs/tools/web.md`

Earlier validation before folding the lint cleanup into this PR:

- `pnpm test:extension google`: 26 test files, 349 tests
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.plugin-sdk.dts.json --declaration true`
- `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs`
- `pnpm lint` on the lint cleanup branch
- `codex review --base origin/main` passed with no introduced actionable correctness issues after the schema/docs update.

Live operational validation before this PR: patched Gemini `web_search(freshness=day)` completed successfully on an OpenClaw gateway, with no Gemini `FIELD_INVALID` / `time_range_filter` errors in the post-deploy journal window.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted: yes. Prepared with Codex. I understand the changed code path: relative Gemini freshness no longer creates a hard `timeRangeFilter`; explicit date ranges still do; cache partitioning keeps freshness-specific responses separate; and the folded lint/type cleanup is mechanical.
